what_it_does: This file is a Vitest integration test for the sync CLI that verifies a single-instance file lock: it creates a temporary project tree, launches the compiled sync-cli.js as child processes under controlled environment variables, and asserts that two concurrent workers coalesce into one real run, that a sequential run is unaffected, that the lock is released on exit, and that a stale lock left by a dead PID is stolen and cleaned up.
observations: Uses both spawnSync and spawn from child_process to drive the CLI.
Creates a temporary directory with projects, archive, config/logs subdirectories.
Writes a minimal JSONL file-history-snapshot so the worker has something to process without network calls.
Passes TEST_PROJECTS_DIR, TEST_ARCHIVE_DIR, TEST_DB_PATH, and TEST_CONFIG_DIR overrides to the CLI.
Explicitly sets EPISODIC_MEMORY_SUMMARIZER_GUARD to undefined in the child environment.
Expects the lock file at config/logs/episodic-memory-sync.lock.
Tests concurrent invocation by spawning two processes and labeling the one that prints 'Sync complete' as the winner.
Stale-lock test writes PID 999999 into the lock file and expects the CLI to replace it and finish.
standouts: A real two-process race is used to test mutual exclusion rather than mocking a lock.
The stale-lock test assumes PID 999999 is not alive on the test machine.
The test fixture is a single zero-exchange JSONL file designed to make the CLI do 'real work' cheaply.
conventions: Uses mkdtempSync and recursive cleanup in afterEach.
Encapsulates CLI invocation in runWith/spawnWith helpers and a collectOutput promise.
concerns: Setting an environment variable to undefined via object spread may not reliably unset it in the child process.
The concurrent test assumes exactly one winner and one loser; if lock behavior is racy the assertion could flake.
The stale-lock test depends on PID 999999 being dead, which is not guaranteed across all environments.
The lock file path is hardcoded in the test rather than derived from a shared constant.
notes: A focused integration test that races two real sync CLI processes to prove the single-instance lock works, releases on exit, and recovers from stale locks.
questions: How does the CLI detect that PID 999999 is dead when stealing the stale lock?
Why must EPISODIC_MEMORY_SUMMARIZER_GUARD be removed from the child environment?
Is the lock file path always under TEST_CONFIG_DIR/logs, or is it configurable in the CLI?
What happens if both spawned processes acquire or skip the lock due to a timing race?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to test the sync-cli single-instance lock (#97), and the file does exactly that by covering concurrency, release, and stale-lock recovery.
apparent_themes: CLI integration testing with real child processes
intention_signals: The file is trying to enforce and verify that the sync CLI cannot run multiple overlapping instances, that duplicate launches exit cleanly, and that a leftover lock from a crashed process does not permanently block future runs.
novelty: It tests a file-based PID lock by actually racing two spawned Node processes and checking that one performs work while the other bails with a specific stderr message.
contribution: This file contributes an executable behavioral contract for the sync CLI's single-instance semantics: it documents how concurrent, sequential, and stale-lock scenarios are expected to behave, which a fused system would keep as living regression coverage for issue #97.
summary_for_embedding: A Vitest integration test that launches the sync CLI in a temporary project tree to verify single-instance file locking, lock release on exit, and stale-lock theft from a dead PID.
intention_for_embedding: To regression-test issue #97 by ensuring the sync CLI never runs more than one instance concurrently and recovers from orphaned lock files.

symbols.does: Declares the shape of a CLI run result used by the test helpers.
symbols.description: A TypeScript interface that records the exit status, captured stdout, and captured stderr returned from running or spawning the sync CLI.

symbols.does: Runs the sync CLI synchronously with the given environment overrides and returns its output.
symbols.description: A helper that calls spawnSync to execute the compiled sync-cli.js under a merged environment, strips the guard variable, enforces a 30-second timeout, and normalizes the result into a RunResult.

symbols.does: Spawns the sync CLI asynchronously with the given environment overrides.
symbols.description: A helper that launches the compiled sync-cli.js as a detached child process with piped stdout/stderr and the same merged, guard-stripped environment used by runWith.

symbols.does: Collects stdout, stderr, and exit code from a spawned child process into a RunResult.
symbols.description: An async helper that drains the piped output streams of a child process, awaits its close event, and returns the captured status, stdout, and stderr.
